title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Go Semantics Case: Rate Limit Identity Algorithm
summary: Concurrent handlers race on a map, launch unbounded goroutines, queue work after clients leave, share one global rate limit, and accept more expensive work than dependencies can sustain. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo rate limit identity algorithm increment in concurrency budget and overload decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Rate Limit Identity Algorithm · Theory Rate Limit Identity Algorithm
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5791 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then drain the old revision while a canary fails its changed-case invariant.
